knowledge_objects.name: Composite Function
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A function formed by applying one function to the result of another.
knowledge_objects.source_span: The composite function h = f ∘ g maps an input x ∈ ℝ^m to f(g(x)).

knowledge_objects.name: Chain Rule
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A rule describing how the derivative of a composite function depends on the derivatives of its constituent functions.
knowledge_objects.source_span: The chain rule describes how the derivative of the composite function depends on the derivatives of f and g.

knowledge_objects.name: Jacobian Matrix
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A matrix collecting all first-order partial derivatives of the output components of a differentiable vector-valued function.
knowledge_objects.source_span: the Jacobian matrix J_g(x) collects all first-order partial derivatives of the output components of g.

knowledge_objects.name: Gradient
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A vector of partial derivatives of a scalar-valued function.

knowledge_objects.name: Multivariable Chain Rule Formula
knowledge_objects.short_definition: ∇h(x) = J_g(x)^⊤ ∇f(g(x)) relates the gradient of the composite function to the Jacobian of g and the gradient of f.
